##NAMES=Pucherite
##RRUFFID=R060824
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Bi(VO_4_)
##LOCALITY=Pucher Shaft, Wolfgang Maaßen mine field, Schneeberg District, Saxony, Germany
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Brown tabular crystals associated with green namibite. Pale yellow crust is also pucherite.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed only by single crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R060824
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.341 b: 5.076 c: 12.065 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 327.09 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
